tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hi! I'm starting to study tort law and I want to keep track of my study preferences and what I'm focusing on so I can pick up where I left off in future sessions. Right now I'm working on understanding the basics of negligence and duty of care. Could you help me save this information?
I'd be happy to help you save your study preferences and current focus! Let me store this information for you.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Tort law study session: Currently focusing on the basics of negligence and duty of care. User wants to track study preferences and pick up where they left off in future sessions."}

messages.content: {"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Tort law study session: Currently focusing on the basics of negligence and duty of care. User wants to track study preferences and pick up where they left off in future sessions."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
Done! I've saved your study progress. Here's what's stored for future reference:

- **Current focus**: Basics of negligence and duty of care
- **Goal**: Track study preferences and pick up where you left off

This will be here when you return to continue studying tort law. Let me know if you want to add more details or update your progress as you go!
Great, thanks for saving that! Could you show me what you just stored? I want to make sure I can find my study notes about negligence and duty of care when I come back to continue studying.

function_call.arguments: {"query": "tort law negligence duty of care study"}

You currently have one memory saved. As you continue your tort law studies, you can add more memories to track your progress, and they'll all show up here. Let me know if you'd like to add any additional notes or study details!

I've been studying for a while now and my focus has shifted from negligence to intentional torts. Could you help me update my stored memory to reflect this change, and remove the old one since it's no longer accurate?
I can help with that! I'll delete your old memory and add a new one with your updated focus on intentional torts.
